Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Analyze Each Case I need to look at each Case ID 1001 1002 1003 1004 and list the activities in chronological order based on the timestamps Then Ill compare this sequence against the standard Procure to Pay P2P process flow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard P2P Flow General Knowledge Create Purchase Requisition PR Approve PR Issue Purchase Order PO Receive Goods GR Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO GR 3 way match ideally Release Payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Constraints PR must be approved before PO PO must be issued before Goods Received usually though blind receipts exist they are less common in standard logs without flags but generally PO precedes GR Goods Received usually precedes or happens around the time of Invoice but definitely before Payment Invoice must be matched before Payment Payment should not happen before Goods Received risk of paying for non delivery Payment should not happen before Invoice Matching risk of paying wrong amount

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Analysis Sequence PR Approve PO GR Invoice Match Payment Close Verdict This looks standard All steps are in logical order No obvious anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Analysis Sequence PR PO Invoice Payment Approve PR GR Match Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Detected Issue PO before Approve PR PO issued at 0840 PR approved at 02 03 0800 This is a major control failure POs should only be issued after PR approval

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment before Goods Received Payment at 02 03 0715 GR at 02 04 0900 Paying before receiving goods is high risk fraud non delivery

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment before Match Payment at 02 03 0715 Match at 02 05 0800 Paying without matching is a control failure overpayment wrong amount

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment before Approve PR Payment happens before the requisition is even approved This suggests the approval is retroactive or bypassed Verdict Highly anomalous Multiple severe control breaches

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Analysis Sequence PR Approve PO Payment Invoice GR Match Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Detected Payment before Invoice Payment at 02 08 Invoice at 02 09 Cannot match or pay without an invoice unless advance payment but usually flagged here it looks like a sequence error

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment before Goods Received Payment at 02 08 GR at 02 10 Risk of paying for non delivery

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment before Match Payment at 02 08 Match at 02 11 Paying without matching Verdict Anomalous Payment occurred too early before receiving the invoice goods or matching This is a significant risk

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Analysis Sequence PR Approve GR PO Invoice Match Payment Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Detected Receive Goods before Issue PO GR at 02 06 0900 PO at 02 06 0915 Goods received before the PO was issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: While Blind Receipts exist receiving goods before a PO is generally a deviation It implies the vendor shipped without a PO or the PO was backdated or created late This breaks the commitment control The PO serves as the legal commitment Receiving goods without a PO can lead to unauthorized purchases or difficulty in matching later Verdict Anomalous Sequence violation regarding PO and GR

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Case 1001 Normal Case 1002 Critical Anomalies PO before Approval Payment before GR Payment before Match Payment before Approval Case 1003 Critical Anomalies Payment before Invoice Payment before GR Payment before Match Case 1004 Moderate Anomaly GR before PO

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure the Response I should organize the response by Case ID for clarity For each anomalous case list the specific anomalies and explain the business risk problem

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Critique Did I mention Match Invoice to PO for Case 1004 Yes Match happened after PO so thats fine The issue is GR before PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Case 1002 in process mining we assume timestamps are accurate reflections of system events If the log says PO issued before Approval its an anomaly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 has Release Payment before Vendor Invoice Received This is impossible in a standard system unless its an advance payment However the log does not indicate Advance Payment In the context of P2P analysis paying before the invoice arrives is a red flag

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Emphasize the Three Way Match concept Standard P2P relies on PO GR and Invoice matching before payment Cases 1002 and 1003 violate this fundamentally

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Output Construction Introduction Briefly state the standard flow for reference Case by Case Analysis Case 1001 Compliant Case 1002 Anomalous list violations and risks Case 1003 Anomalous list violations and risks Case 1004 Anomalous list violations and risks Overall Summary 1002 and 1003 are high risk 1004 is a process deviation 1001 is clean
